source.title: Obstructive sleep apnoea/hypopnoea syndrome and obesity hypoventilation syndrome in over 16s
source.population: People aged over 16 years with suspected or diagnosed obstructive sleep apnoea/hypopnoea syndrome.
source.setting: Initial assessment, sleep-service referral, diagnostic testing, treatment discussion, and follow-up.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected adult OSAHS symptom, assessment, sleep-study, referral, driving-risk, treatment-discussion, and monitoring fields; not applicable to children and not directly validated for UAE sleep-service or driving pathways.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports snoring, witnessed apnoeas, unrefreshing or fragmented sleep, waking headache, sleepiness or fatigue, nocturia, choking, cognitive symptoms, obesity context, Epworth Sleepiness Scale, and STOP-Bang, while prohibiting use of Epworth alone to determine referral.

items.final_wording: Supports referral information on assessment scores, functional sleepiness, comorbidities, occupational or driving risk, and oxygen saturation or blood gases if available.

source.title: Obstructive sleep apnoea/hypopnoea syndrome and obesity hypoventilation syndrome in over 16s

items.rationale: Supports prioritising qualified sleep-service assessment for snoring and sleep-disordered breathing review; no decision is generated.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician-reviewed respiratory polygraphy, home oximetry where appropriate, polysomnography in the specified context, and use of sleep-study results by clinicians to diagnose and grade OSAHS.
